I woke up late on Tuesday, scrambled to make coffee, and barely caught the bus. Work was hectic — three meetings back-to-back, then a surprise fire drill that lasted twenty minutes. Lunch? A sad granola bar at my desk.

But the afternoon turned around. My manager praised the prototype I'd been sweating over for weeks. 'Ship it,' she said, just like that. I practically floated home.

Sarah called in the evening to vent about her landlord raising rent again. We talked for an hour, swapping war stories. By 10 PM I was asleep on the couch with a half-read novel on my chest.
